name: Macadamia Shortbread Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to the high position, about one-third to one-quarter down from the top.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
3. Place a flat-sided cookie sheet or any sheet turned upside down on the baking tray.
4. Line the sheet with parchment paper or foil, shiny side up.
5. In a small bowl, beat butter until soft using an electric mixer.
6. Beat in vanilla and almond extracts, salt, and sugar. Then beat in the egg and mix slowly on low speed until incorporated.
7. Remove from the mixer and stir in the nuts with a spatula. Work with half the dough at a time.
8. Place it by heaping tablespoons into a strip about 9 inches long down the middle of a piece of plastic wrap (15 inches long).
9. Lift the two long sides of the wrap and bring them together on top of the strip. Shape the strip into a smooth loaf, about 9 inches long, 2 inches wide, and 1 inch thick.
10. Turn the loaf upside down and shape the other side. Repeat with the second half of dough to create a second length of plastic wrap.
11. Place them in the freezer for 10 to 12 minutes (no longer) so they become non-sticky enough to be unwrapped.
12. Unwrap the dough packages and place them across the sheet, not lengthwise, with plenty of space in the middle and at the ends. They will spread out to about 4 inches wide.
13. Bake for 25 minutes, reversing the sheet front to back once during baking. When done, the tops will be pale with golden spots, and the edges slightly darker. Remove from the oven.
14. Reduce temperature to 300°F.
15. Carefully slide the parchment off the sheet onto a cutting board. If the edges are too dark or uneven, trim them slightly using a long and sharp knife.
16. Let loaves rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
17. Cut the loaves crosswise or at an angle into slices 3/4 to 1 inch wide with a long, thin, sharp knife.
18. Place the slices upright on the cookie sheet.
19. When the oven has reduced temperature to 300°F, bake for another 25 minutes, reversing the sheet front to back once during baking. Remove slices as they are ready.
20. Place baked slices on a brown paper bag or double thickness of paper towels to cool. Handle carefully and store in an airtight container.
